items.title: Khaled says government to assess new opposition leader Samsuri
items.summary: MUAR: The government will monitor the performance of Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his new role as opposition leader, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. He said the government would observe and assess performance in all positions, including the opposition leader. Samsuri, who is Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu menteri besar, will replace Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader.

items.title: Rafizi, Nik Nazmi take over Parti Bersama Malaysia
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today took over Parti Bersama Malaysia. The party, also known as the Malaysian United Party, was established in 2016. Also present were several members of parliament, namely Wong Chen (Subang), Datuk Bakhtiar Wan Chik (Balik Pulau), Rodziah Ismail (Ampang), Zahir Hassan (Wangsa Maju) and Lee Chean Chung (Petaling Jaya).

items.title: Pua questions PH-BN pact after Johor seat move
items.summary: The former Damansara member of parliament said Umno's decision to contest all 56 Johor state seats in the upcoming state election had exposed the party's "true colours". "By declaring their intent to contest all 56 state seats in Johor in the upcoming state elections, Umno has revealed its true colours, wanting its cake and eating it too. He said PH had done everything possible to maintain political stability under the Unity government, despite what he described as an "awkward arrangement" with BN following the 2022 general election.

items.title: Rafizi Ramli, Nik Nazmi take over Malaysian United Party
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 17 — A year after their departure from the Cabinet, PKR’s Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today announced that they will take over the Malaysian United Party (MUP) as their new political platform. The announcement was made today at the PJ Performing Arts Centre in a symbolic handover ceremony. MUP’s founder and president, Tan Gin Theam, presented the two PKR stalwarts with a mock party registration certificate to officiate the transition.

items.title: Nearly 60pct of Malaysian workers overweight or obese
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 60 per cent of Malaysian workers who underwent health screenings were found to be overweight or obese, revealing a worrying health trend in the workforce. Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the 59.2 per cent figure was significantly high and could not be taken lightly. Also present were Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy and international) and acting secretary-general Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar, Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail, Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (operations) Sutekno Ahmad Belon, Perkeso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal and Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.

items.title: Proton strengthens market presence in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd has strengthened its position in Sabah and Sarawak, with sales volume rising 283 per cent since 2018. The national carmaker said sales in Sabah and Sarawak increased from 7,697 units in 2018 to 29,461 units in 2025, driven by sustained demand for models such as the Proton Saga, Proton X50 and Proton X70. Meanwhile, Proton launched its 10,000 sMILEs Expedition, a cross-border convoy covering Sabah, Sarawak and Brunei as part of its efforts to further strengthen engagement with customers in Sabah and Sarawak.

items.title: A day after BN, Johor PH says it will contest all 56 seats too
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) today announced it will contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election. State PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan said the decision follows a declaration yesterday by Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi that his coalition would contest all state seats. "The Johor menteri besar has stated that the state BN will contest 56 seats, so Johor PH will also contest all 56 seats.

items.title: UK seeks greater trade, investment growth with Malaysia: High Commissioner
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The United Kingdom (UK) aims to increase its trade with Malaysia this year after recording an 11 per cent growth in 2025, bringing the total bilateral trade to 6.5 billion pounds (RM34.26 billion), said the British High Commissioner to Malaysia Ajay Sharma. "The UK and Malaysia have a really strong, historic and multi-dimensional relationship. "You (Malaysia) have a skilled workforce.

items.title: Foreign-linked funding claims against Mandiri: Let authorities investigate, says Fahmi
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Allegations involving purported foreign-linked funding connected to youth-based non-governmental organisation Mandiri should be left to the authorities to investigate and verify, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today. The PKR information chief, who was responding to allegations raised by the party's youth wing, Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK), said the government would allow enforcement agencies to determine the authenticity of claims circulating online. Amid the growing political storm, Mandiri denied allegations linking the organisation to foreign interference or any plot to topple Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: KLIA police nab Chinese national who barged past autogates after burning through travel funds
items.summary: SEPANG, May 17 — A woman from China was detained after behaving aggressively while attempting to pass through the international departure gate at Terminal 1 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) yesterday. KLIA Police Chief ACP M. Ravi said in the 1.35 pm incident at International Departure Gate C, the suspect was found passing through the security inspection autogate without a valid ticket and acted aggressively when approached by security personnel for inspection. “Acting on information received, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) KLIA auxiliary police detained the woman at 1.45 pm on the same day,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Chinese national arrested for trespass, alleged disruption at KLIA Terminal 1 [WATCH]
items.summary: SEPANG: Police have arrested a Chinese national for trespassing into a restricted security area at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1. The woman is also believed to have acted aggressively towards security personnel after entering the restricted area without a flight ticket, police said. KLIA police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the incident occurred at 1.35pm on May 16 at the international departure gate C of Terminal 1.

items.title: Big risks and rewards in upcoming IPOs at SpaceX, OpenAI, Anthropic
items.summary: NEW YORK: Wall Street is licking its chops over an unprecedented slate of massive initial public offerings (IPOs) set to arrive in the coming months, beginning with Elon Musk's SpaceX in June. SpaceX is targeting an initial public offering that would raise up to US$80 billion, roughly double the funds generated from all 2025 IPOs. OpenAI and Anthropic are eyeing IPOs raising US$60 billion, also huge numbers compared with the norm.

items.title: DayOne plans dual IPO in Singapore and US, FT reports
items.summary: HONG KONG: Global data centre operator DayOne, affiliated with China's GDS Holdings, is planning to list simultaneously in Singapore and the US in a move that is set to raise US$5 billion, the Financial Times reported on Sunday. Reuters could not immediately confirm the report. Reuters reported in February that DayOne planned to raise US$5 billion in a US initial public offering, citing a source with knowledge of the matter, adding the company could be valued at US$20 billion in the IPO.

items.title: Civil Defence Force chief calls on personnel to remain adaptive, proactive during South-west Monsoon
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — Malaysia Civil Defence Force (APM) personnel have been urged to remain prepared for any eventualities during the current South-west Monsoon phase. APM chief commissioner Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman said they must remain adaptive and proactive by leveraging their training and experience to ensure a swift response when needed. He said this while speaking at the Federal Territories (FT) APM assembly here today, which was also attended by FT Civil Defence director Mohd Rosman Abdullah.

items.title: Senior policeman arrested over alleged extortion in Bukit Bintang
items.summary: He said the suspect was remanded for four days beginning May 16 to assist in investigations. The alleged incident occurred at 1.30am on March 14 at a restaurant in Jalan Angsoka, Bukit Bintang. Sazalee said the complainant claimed his colleague was detained by a group of policemen after exiting a car.

items.title: Raja Muda of Perlis leads list of 24 state award recipients
items.summary: The Perlis state government said in a statement that Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin consented to confer the Darjah Kebesaran Seri Setia Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail (SSSJ), which carries the title Datuk Seri Diraja, on Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra. The Ruler also consented to confer the honours on Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil and Tengku Puteri Mahkota of Perlis, Datuk Seri Datin Paduka Hajah Sharifah Fazira Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail. Shamsul Azri was awarded the Darjah Seri Paduka Mahkota Perlis (SPMP), which carries the title Datuk Seri.

items.title: Couple claims trial to charge of abusing 10‑year‑old nephew in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 17 — A married couple pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of abusing their nephew last April. Wan Saiful Azuan Wan Idris, 31, and Yujas Nor Aida Jasmin Mohd Yusoff, 29, entered the plea after the charge was read out to them before Judge Zulkifli Abllah. — Bernama * If you suspect child abuse, call the following hotlines for free and confidential support: Talian Kasih at 15999 or WhatsApp 019-2615999 (24/7); Talian BuddyBear at 1800-18-2327(BEAR) (noon-midnight daily); and One Crisis Centre (24/7) Wilayah Persekutuan at 03-26155555 (Kuala Lumpur General Hospital), 03-61454333 (Sungai Buloh Hospital) or 03-83124200 (Putrajaya Hospital).

items.title: BN-PH alliance still intact, says Fahmi, denying Johor BN's solo move would jeopardise ties
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) remained prepared for any political possibility, including growing speculation of an early Johor state election, while insisting cooperation between PH and Barisan Nasional (BN) in the federal unity government remained intact. "Johor PH had already conveyed its position following Johor BN's announcement that it intended to contest all 56 state seats in the next state election. Asked whether Johor BN's move could jeopardise PH-BN cooperation, Fahmi rejected the notion outright.

items.title: A Proton Saga sparked a lifelong passion for local innovation
items.summary: I was in Standard Six at Clifford Primary School, Kuala Kangsar, back in 1985, waiting for Mak to pick me up. Panic started to set in, but I tried to stay calm, hoping she would appear at any moment. By car, the trip took twenty minutes.

items.title: Anwar congratulates Perlis ruler Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin on 83rd birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has conveyed his congratulations to the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, on the occasion of His Royal Highness’s 83rd birthday today. In a Facebook post, Anwar prayed that Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Almarhum Tengku Abdul Rashid, and the Perlis royal family would continue to be blessed with Allah SWT’s mercy and guidance, and enjoy good health and well-being. “Daulat Tuanku,” he said.

items.title: Mindef sets up three committees after Norway cancels missile export licence
items.summary: MUAR: The Defence Ministry has established three committees following Norway's cancellation of export licences for the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) anti-ship missile system to Malaysia. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the committees would focus on contractual decision-making, exploring alternative systems, and seeking a diplomatic resolution. Following the move by the Norwegian Defence Ministry, Malaysia protested, labelling the decision unilateral and unacceptable.

items.title: Pakatan to field candidates in all 56 state seats in upcoming Johor polls after BN move, says Aminolhuda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) today announced that it will contest in all 56 state constituencies in the upcoming polls, following Barisan Nasional’s (BN) announcement yesterday. Johor PH chief Aminolhuda Hassan said the decision was made after BN Johor announced that they would contest in all state seats on their own. He said Johor PH will not back down on this decision and dismissed any cooperation with BN for the state polls.

items.title: First batch of TVET students head to China for AI, engineering training
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-five students under the Malaysia-China Youth TVET Training (MCYTT) 2.0 programme for 2026 departed for China today to undergo training in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Technical Mechanical fields. The students form the first batch of participants under the initiative, marking the official launch of the latest edition of the international Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) mobility programme. The programme is organised by the National Skilled Workers Organisation (Belia Mahir) in collaboration with TANG International Education Group, with the Malaysian Indian Youth Council (MIYC) serving as a strategic partner.

items.title: 'Second Dubai' vision not impossible for Perlis, says Raja of Perlis
items.summary: ARAU: The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, has envisioned the state developing rapidly into a "Second Dubai" in the future. "Perlis too can become like Dubai one day. "But today, many tourists come specifically to Perlis.

items.title: 'Please don't betray us,' Aminuddin tells PH members
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has described the recent political crisis in the state as an act of betrayal by "friends" who had previously shown loyalty to the Pakatan Harapan (PH) administration. Aminuddin, who is also Negri Sembilan PH chairman, said although the issue had been resolved, it had left behind the scars of betrayal. "In Negri Sembilan, we have a large ship.

items.title: Minister: Defence Literacy Programme launched under Jiwa Murni Programme to boost public awareness on geopolitics
items.summary: MUAR, May 17 — The Defence Literacy Programme will be introduced as a new component under the Ministry of Defence’s Jiwa Murni Programme (PJM) this year, said Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin. He said the programme, run by the Malaysian Institute of Defence and Security (MiDAS), aims to educate the public on geopolitics as part of the Comprehensive Defence (Hanruh) strategy. During the event, Mohamed Khaled presented a cheque for RM6 million for the 2025 Kementah Madani PJM implementation.

items.title: Indonesian couple among 22 migrants fined over immigration offences
items.summary: PASIR MAS: An Indonesian couple were among 22 undocumented migrants charged with immigration offences at the Sessions Court here today. All 22 pleaded guilty before Sessions Court Judge Zulkifli Abdullah. Thirteen were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63 for overstaying, seven under Section 6(1)(c) for entering without valid travel documents, and five under Regulation 39(b) of the Immigration Regulations 1963 for misuse of passes.

items.title: MPS urges government to drop appeal against liquid nicotine ruling
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Pharmacists Society (MPS) has urged the government not to appeal a High Court ruling which found the exemption of liquid and gel nicotine from the Poisons Act 1952 to be irrational and procedurally flawed. MPS president Amrahi Buang said the government should accept the court's decision to strengthen public health governance and protect the younger generation from nicotine addiction. He said immediate steps must be taken to ensure liquid and gel nicotine used in vape and electronic cigarettes are placed back under appropriate poison controls.

items.title: Philippines asks top court to allow ICC drug war arrest of senator
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippines said it has asked the country's Supreme Court to allow it to arrest ex-president Rodrigo Duterte's chief drug war enforcer to stand trial in an international tribunal. The International Criminal Court last week unsealed an arrest warrant against Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, accusing him along with Duterte and other "co-perpetrators" of the "crime against humanity of murder." Dela Rosa briefly sought refuge in the Senate last week while asking the Philippine Supreme Court to stop an ongoing attempt by government agents to arrest him.

items.title: Nine MA63 issues resolved in three years under Madani govt, strongest record yet, says Mustapha Sakmud
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Madani Government has achieved its best record in the nation’s history by resolving nine matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) within just three years of administration. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the achievement surpassed earlier administrations, which managed to resolve only four matters over a longer period. “We have resolved nine matters within three years together with both state governments, with the participation of the Premier of Sarawak and the Chief Minister of Sabah.

items.title: Penang Pakatan-BN ties ‘strong and stable’, says Chow Kon Yeow amid Johor rift
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today affirmed that the political alliance between Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) in his state remains “strong and stable,” drawing a sharp contrast with the political developments in Johor. Speaking at the 2026 PH Convention here today, Chow insisted that Penang has prioritised political stability to safeguard the state’s future for its people, investors, and youth. “The people at present want stability, investors want stability, and young people looking for jobs and a future also want stability.” Chow, who also serves as the Penang PH chairman, explained his governance style, saying that he has opted to make responsible, albeit sometimes unpopular, decisions over populist moves to protect the state’s long-term interests.

items.title: Socso health screening shows 59.2pc of Malaysian workers overweight or obese, 19pc have diabetes, says deputy minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 17 — A total of 59.2 per cent of workers who underwent the Health Screening Programme organised by the Social Security Organisation (Socso) between 2023 and 2025 were found to be overweight or obese, said Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan. He said the findings also showed that about 19 per cent of them had diabetes, while 17.45 per cent suffered from high blood pressure. Also present Human Resources Ministry (KESUMA) deputy secretary-general (Policy and International), who is carrying out the duties of KESUMA secretary-general, Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar; Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail; Socso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal; and Socso Group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.

items.title: 'Johor is a BN govt': Khaled Nordin says no need for talks with federal leaders over solo move
items.summary: MUAR: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) decided to contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election because the state administration is not a unity government. "All parties must understand that the Johor state government is a BN government. In the 15th Johor state election in March 2022, BN formed the state government after winning 40 out of 56 state seats.

items.title: Kok Lanas rep to retain state seat despite Bersatu sacking
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kok Lanas assemblyman Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi will retain his seat despite being sacked from Bersatu, says Kelantan State Legislative Assembly Speaker Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah. Amar said Farid's situation differed from the previous Nenggiri state seat vacancy that triggered a by-election. Even if Bersatu informs us, his position as an assemblyman remains unaffected," Amar said.

items.title: Riyadh intensifies awareness on visiting prophet's mosque etiquettes
items.summary: MADINAH: Saudi Arabia's General Presidency for Religious Affairs at the Grand Mosque and the Prophet's Mosque has intensified awareness and guidance efforts to educate pilgrims and visitors on the etiquette of visiting the Prophet's Mosque and the proper manner of greeting the Prophet and his two companions. According to Saudi Press Agency (SPA), the efforts are being carried out through an integrated system of guidance tools and enriching content presented in several languages, enhancing the religious and intellectual awareness of visitors during the haj season. The awareness programmes include delivering direct guidance messages within the Prophet's Mosque and its courtyards, in addition to utilising digital screens, awareness publications, and multilingual digital content.

items.title: Couple deny abusing nephew in Kelantan court case
items.summary: The accused, Wan Saiful Azuan Wan Idris, 31, and Yujas Nor Aida Jasmin Mohd Yusoff, 29, however, denied the charge before Judge Zulkifli Abdullah. According to the charge, the couple allegedly acted together in abusing the victim, causing injury. Nur Syafiqah objected to bail, saying the offence was serious.

items.title: Pakatan convention to chart new strategies for nation’s future, says Fahmi
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) 2026 Convention held today serves as the best platform for component parties of the coalition to formulate new strategies and approaches to ensure a brighter future for the country, said Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. The PH 2026 Convention director said the convention, which is being held again after four years, provides delegates with the opportunity to pool ideas and energy for the benefit of Malaysians. He said this in his welcoming speech at the PH 2026 Convention at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here.

items.title: Vatican gets serious about AI with new commission
items.summary: ROME: The Vatican is to establish a commission to address the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI), reported German news agency dpa. Pope Leo XIV has approved the creation of the Inter-Dicasterial Commission on Artificial Intelligence to examine the "potential effects on human beings and on humanity as a whole" of AI. Representatives of several Vatican bodies are to sit on the commission, the Holy See said.

items.title: Law can't replace love for parents
items.summary: LETTERS: The proposal by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi to consider introducing legislation compelling children to care for their parents is, in many ways, deeply troubling and unnecessary. It is sad and rather unbelievable that in a society built upon family values, love, sacrifice, and gratitude, may now require laws to compel children to fulfil what should naturally be a moral duty and responsibility. Respect and care for parents are values that must be cultivated from a young age through education, family upbringing, moral teachings, and societal values.

items.title: Americans 'should demonstrate like the French,' says Woody Harrelson
items.summary: CANNES: US actor Woody Harrelson told AFP that Americans should "demonstrate like the French" because "we let our government get away with so much" as he premiered a new black comedy Saturday at the Cannes Film Festival. "The French are like, no way. Harrelson, who famously said that he "felt dirty" after spending an evening with US President Donald Trump two decades ago, said "this whole world's going to hell in a handbasket."

items.title: Johor poll speculation grows on WhatsApp claims of June 24 assembly sitting
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Speculation over a possible Johor snap state election has intensified following viral WhatsApp messages alleging a special state assembly sitting on June 24. Johor BN state liaison secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said no official statement has been issued on the matter so far. Political speculation in Johor intensified after Menteri Besar and Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced yesterday that BN Johor would contest all 56 state seats in the next state election.

items.title: Kelantan peatlands under threat from open burning, land clearing; Fire Dept warns of flash flood risk
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 17 — Peatlands in Kelantan, which serve as natural water catchment areas, are increasingly threatened by open burning and land clearing for agricultural activities, raising concerns over a higher risk of flash floods in several parts of the state. State Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Farhan Sufyan Borhan said peatlands play a crucial role in storing water naturally due to their ability to absorb large volumes of water during heavy rainfall. He said most peatland areas in Kelantan are located in Bachok, Pasir Puteh and Pasir Mas, which are also identified as high-risk areas for open burning during the dry season.

items.title: Sarawak tourism minister hails international deep sea fishing tournament as major tourism event for Miri
items.summary: MIRI, May 17 — Events such as the Miri City International Deep Sea Fishing Tournament not only promote sports fishing and tourism, but also strengthen friendship, cultural exchange and international cooperation among participants from different countries. In this regard, Minister of Tourism, Creative Industry and Performing Arts Sarawak Datuk Seri Abdul Karim Rahman Hamzah hailed the tournament as an important tourism event for Miri, and Sarawak in general. “This clearly shows that international interest in Miri’s fishing tourism remains strong and continues to grow.

items.title: Johor Pakatan chief says BN has ‘closed the door’ on political cooperation for state polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — The state’s Pakatan Harapan (PH) considers the prospect of a political alliance with Barisan Nasional (BN) for the next state election to be effectively over, said chairman Aminolhuda Hassan The Johor PH chief said Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi’s yesterday announcement that it would contest all seats in the state independently was essentially “closing the door” on any electoral pact. “We take this as Johor BN declaring that there will be no cooperation with PH,” he added during a press conference at the 2026 PH Convention, held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre today. He added that Johor BN’s decision yesterday had also indirectly determined the state’s PH political direction ahead of the convention and the upcoming state election.

items.title: More than 5,000 join Niti Daun parade for Bintulu Gawai Dayak celebration
items.summary: BINTULU, May 17 — A total of 73 contingents comprising more than 5,000 participants took part in the ‘Niti Daun’ parade at the esplanade here yesterday, held in conjunction with the Bintulu Division Gawai Dayak 2026 celebration. The Public Works Department (JKR) Bintulu Division, as the main organiser, said the annual event highlighted the spirit of unity among Bintulu’s diverse communities. Also present were Bintulu Resident Datu Nyurak Keti, Bintulu District officer Awang Mohammed Nizam Awang Ali Bohan, and JKR Bintulu divisional engineer Lau Sie Hung.

items.title: Marine Police seize RM2.95m frozen meat, diesel allegedly without permits in Bintulu, Julau
items.summary: KUCHING, May 17 — Sarawak Marine Police Region 5 seized frozen meat and diesel allegedly without valid permits, with a total estimated value of about RM2.95 million, during two separate operations in Bintulu and Julau on Friday. He said inspection of the premises led to the discovery of eight containers, four of which contained approximately 43,849.60 kilogrammes of frozen meat. Ab Rahman said the total value of seizures from both cases amounted to RM2,947,980.

items.title: AP x Swatch ‘Royal Pop’ Fever: Swatch urges calm, caps queue sizes after chaotic launch including in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Following a weekend of frenzied queues and arguments, Swatch has issued an official plea to fans to stop rushing its stores for the new Audemars Piguet (AP) x Swatch collaboration. The “Royal Pop” collection, which officially launched yesterday, saw hundreds of hopeful buyers lining up overnight at key locations in Kuala Lumpur. Swatch X Audemars Piguet Royal Pop watches are displayed in a presentation case at Mid Valley Megamall in Kuala Lumpur on May 16, 2026.

items.title: Workload, social media pressures add to teachers’ mental health challenges, says Kelantan Social Welfare Dept
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 17 — The teaching profession has become increasingly challenging in the digital era, with teachers facing not only teaching and administrative duties, but also pressure from social media and viral issues that can affect their emotional wellbeing and mental health. Kelantan Social Welfare Department (JKM) director Che Samsuzuki Che Noh said advances in technology and social media had exposed teachers to greater psychological pressure, as issues involving schools could easily attract public attention. He said the situation required more comprehensive emotional and psychological support to help teachers adapt to current challenges.

items.title: PACE to boost national talent development with RM100m allocation to strengthen workforce skills, says Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The Ministry of Human Resources (KESUMA) will launch a new national initiative, Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employability (PACE), with a total allocation of RM100 million to strengthen human capital development and enhance the employability of the country’s workforce. Its Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the initiative, implemented through the Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp), is among the government’s strategic efforts to ensure that the people possess skills relevant to current and future industry needs, while strengthening the country’s competitiveness at the global level. PACE will introduce 10 strategic programmes spanning Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), youth talent development, digital economy transformation, strengthening local enterprises, regional development, and improving the employability of gig workers and inclusive communities.

items.title: SUV driver aged 75 arrested over Klang hit-and-run that killed two sisters
items.summary: KLANG, May 17 — A 75-year-old man has been arrested in connection with a tragic accident that claimed the lives of two sisters in Bandar Baru Klang last Thursday. The siblings, aged 57 and 62, were reportedly walking to dinner with a 17-year-old boy when they were struck by a sport utility vehicle (SUV) along Jalan Pekan Baru. Klang Utara district police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao confirmed that the local man was detained at approximately 11.40 am on Friday to assist with investigations.

items.title: Pakatan convention kicks off with 5,000 delegates, Anwar to deliver keynote this afternoon
items.summary: Prime Minister and PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to deliver the keynote address and officiate the convention’s main event later this afternoon. With the theme “Tekad Madani, Harapan Rakyat”, the one-day convention began at 8.30am with a town hall session involving PH wings, followed by speeches from PH state chairmen. Among leaders scheduled to speak are Democratic Action Party (DAP) secretary-general Anthony Loke, Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, and PH secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail.

items.title: Two colleagues killed after Honda City bursts into flames in Jalan Sungai Besi crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — A man and a woman were killed early this morning after their car veered off the road and crashed into a concrete pillar, causing the vehicle to burst into flames along Jalan Sungai Besi. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (JSPT) chief, Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa, said the accident occurred at approximately 3:01 am, New Straits Times reported. The car skidded and slammed into a concrete pillar on the left side of the road before catching fire,” Mohd Zamzuri said.

items.title: Al-Sultan Abdullah: Kampung Bantal close to family’s heart, may become Pahang’s first Royal Village
items.summary: JERANTUT, May 17 — The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, has expressed his desire for Kampung Bantal in Ulu Tembeling to be designated as the state’s first Royal Village. This will be the first Royal Village in Pahang. Also gracing the event was Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah.

items.title: MIC, MCA back BN move to contest all 56 Johor seats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Barisan Nasional (BN) component parties MCA and MIC have expressed full support for the coalition’s decision to contest all 56 state seats in the upcoming Johor state election. Johor MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon said the decision announced yesterday by Johor BN chairman and Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was a bold, accurate and far-sighted move to ensure BN continues to lead Johor with stability. He said since BN formed the government with a two-thirds majority in 2022, Johor under Onn Hafiz’s leadership had recorded excellent economic performance as a result of the political stability enjoyed by the state.

items.title: ‘Does PAS have no more leaders?’ Guan Eng questions Samsuri’s ‘two hats’ as Opposition Leader and MB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 —DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng has questioned the depth of leadership within PAS following the appointment of Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as the new Opposition Leader. Lim expressed scepticism over Ahmad Samsuri’s ability to manage his duties effectively, citing the significant geographical distance between the administrative hub of Kuala Lumpur and the Terengganu state capital, Kuala Terengganu. Lim also highlighted Ahmad Samsuri’s current parliamentary attendance record as the MP for Kemaman to support his concerns.

items.title: PM’s political secretary hands sacrificial cattle donations to enforcement bodies, community groups in Kuantan
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 17 — Government agencies, enforcement bodies, mosques, suraus and community organisations in the Indera Mahkota parliamentary constituency and Kuantan received donations of sacrificial cattle in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha here yesterday. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s political secretary Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi said the contributions were intended to help ensure the smooth implementation of this year’s korban rituals while strengthening ukhuwah, or brotherhood, within the community. The donations were presented at the Sacrificial Cattle Donation Presentation Ceremony for the Indera Mahkota parliamentary constituency and Kuantan at Masjid Tengku Abdullah Al Haj, Chengal Lempong.

items.title: Pudu parking touts sentenced to jail and fines following DBKL crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Three Malaysian men have been sentenced to jail and fined after being caught illegally collecting parking fees in the Pudu area. The arrests were made during Kuala Lumpur City Hall’s (DBKL) “Jaga” special operations conducted on Thursday. The operation targeted hotspots along Jalan Yew and Jalan Pasar Baharu following numerous public complaints regarding unauthorised “jaga kereta (parking tout)” activities.

items.title: Police detain 75‑year‑old man in Klang hit‑and‑run after SUV crash kills two, injures teen
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 17 — Police detained a 75-year-old man believed to be linked to a hit-and-run incident involving three pedestrians at Jalan Pekan Baru, Bandar Baru Klang, on Friday. North Klang police chief ACP S Vijaya Rao said the man was arrested at about 11.40am yesterday following information received from the public over the incident, which left two people dead and another seriously injured. Yesterday, Vijaya said two sisters died while a 17-year-old teenager was injured after a sport utility vehicle (SUV) crashed into three pedestrians in the suspected hit-and-run incident on May 15.

items.title: Housing minister: Malaysia committed to advancing resilient, inclusive cities via UN‑Habitat collaboration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — Malaysia is looking to strengthen cooperation with the United Nations Human Settlements Programme (UN-Habitat) in urban liveability, digital urbanisation and Voluntary Local Reviews (VLRs) as part of its commitment to advancing resilient and inclusive cities amid growing geopolitical uncertainties. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming arrived in Baku, Azerbaijan, yesterday for an official working visit, reflecting Malaysia’s continued engagement in global urban diplomacy as countries navigate increasingly complex challenges affecting cities and communities worldwide. As a long-standing advocate of sustainable urbanisation, Nga said Malaysia would continue prioritising affordable housing under its national development agenda, alongside policies promoting low-carbon cities, improved local governance and the digital transformation of urban services.

items.title: MMEA: SAR ends for Pulau Pangkor migrant boat tragedy, 23 survivors, 16 dead
items.summary: IPOH, May 17 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for victims of the boat capsizing incident involving Indonesian migrants in waters off Pulau Pangkor on May 11 was officially called off yesterday after six days. Perak Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) in a statement today said that the operation ended at 7 pm following no new discoveries in the search area. “Initial information obtained on the first day of the operation stated that the total number of victims was 37, however the figure is believed to be inaccurate,” the statement said.
